Operation 'executeaction' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary 'Execute Zapier Action' is too generic and doesn't indicate what actions are available. Description uses vague language ('various actions') without specifying which actions are supported or their effects. Missing critical details about required parameters, authentication, return values, and side effects. The description doesn't help AI agents understand when to use this operation or what to expect. Improved version specifies the three supported actions (get leads, subscribe webhooks, unsubscribe webhooks), clarifies authentication requirements, explains the action parameter's role, and describes expected outputs for each action type to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Execute various Zapier actions including getting recent leads, subscribing/unsubscribing webhooks.
data.summary_suggestion: Execute webhook or lead retrieval action
data.description_suggestion: Executes specified Zapier actions for webhook and lead management. Supports three action types: retrieving recent leads from the CRM, subscribing to lead webhooks, or unsubscribing from webhooks. Requires API key authentication. Returns action-specific response data (leads array, webhook subscription confirmation, or unsubscribe confirmation). The action parameter determines which operation is performed and what data is returned.
